about: Located in the Inner Castle of Harput and built in the 12th century by Belek Ghazi and his successors, the Artuqid Palace is a unique structure reflecting the grandeur of its era. The surviving ruins—with their wide inner courtyards, iwan architecture, and civil design showcasing the luxurious daily life of the castle's administrators—make it one of the most important architectural masterpieces in the region. This palace served not only as an administrative center but also as a hub supporting science and art.
